topic: Residual sugar and low alcohol for spicy-food pairing
trigger_keywords: vino con dulzor residual y baja graduación
residual sugar low alcohol chili pairing
sweet low-alcohol wine with spicy food

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Chili-containing food creates a persistent burning sensation through capsaicin, and wine alcohol can add a separate warming sensation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Choosing a lower-alcohol wine avoids reinforcing that alcoholic heat, while some residual sugar can soften the contrast and make the combination feel less severe without neutralising capsaicin.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: A wine with modest alcohol and some residual sugar usually gives a more comfortable and balanced pairing with spicy food than a highly alcoholic or strongly tannic wine.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: with spicy food, favour some residual sugar and low alcohol because sweetness can soften the contrast while low alcohol avoids intensifying heat.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
